question.text: When a company announces corporate action, short deliveries of cum-basis shares shall be:

answer.text: Directly closed out if auction pay-out cannot be done on or before Record Date/Book closure minus 1 business day

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Cum-basis short deliveries are directly closed out if auction pay-out can't happen before Record Date minus 1 day. Close-out is at highest price or 10% above settlement price, whichever is higher. **The Trap:** Note the lower 10% premium for corporate action close-outs vs the regular 20%. **Cross-Exam Context:** Corporate action close-outs have time sensitivity due to record dates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The tighter timeline around corporate actions necessitates direct close-out rather than auction.
